svg.text: Variables
I. Custom Variables: Definition & Types
Custom variables are data structures stored in an entity's custom variable component.
They use custom variable names (string type) as unique identifiers within the same entity.
Supported data types: Integer, Float, String, Boolean, 3D Vector, Entities, GUID,
Prefab ID, Configuration ID, Structure, and their List variants, plus Dictionary types.
Life Cycle: Follows the entity. Variables are destroyed when the entity is destroyed.
Scope: Global for both reading and writing — any entity can access any other entity's variables.
II. Custom Variable Component & Editing
The custom variable component is the data carrier. An entity must have this component before
custom variable operations can be performed. All units have this component by default.
In the Advanced Editing tab, you can view, add, and modify custom variables including their
names, types, and default values. The Stage Overview lets you view all variables on stage.
Caution: Changing a variable's name or type after use in node graphs may cause nodes to fail.
III. Custom Variable Node Graph Nodes
Set Custom Variable
Node Type: Execute
Sets a named custom variable on the target entity. Creates a new variable if none exists.
Supports generic input. Parameters: Target Entity, Variable Name, Value, Trigger Event.
Get Custom Variable
Node Type: Query
Retrieves a custom variable value by name from the target entity. The output is generic and
requires connection or manual type specification before use.
When Custom Variable Changes
Node Type: Event
Triggers when any custom variable on the entity changes. Fires even if the value remains
identical. Filter by variable name for specific monitoring.
Outputs: Event Source Entity, Source GUID, Variable Name, Pre-Change Value, Post-Change Value
Note: Container types (List, Dictionary, Structure) omit pre/post change values.
IV. Node Graph Variables: Definition & Scope
Node Graph Variables have a life cycle that follows the node graph, and are only valid in
Server Node Graphs. They can only be accessed within the node graph they reside in.
Why use them: Lightweight, allow duplicate names across graphs, support modular design.
Aspect
Node Graph Variables
Custom Variables
Life Cycle
Follows Node Graph
Follows Entity
Scope
Within node graph only
Global (any entity)
Naming & Override
Unique in graph, expose to stage
Unique in entity, general override
V. Node Graph Variables: Editing & Exposing
Access the Node Graph Variables tab from the node graph editing shortcut menu.
Create and edit variables directly in the tab. Drag a variable into the editing area to quickly
create Set or Get operation nodes. Click the Expose button to allow stage-level
value overwriting on different entity instances for the same Node Graph Variable.
Container Type Pass-by-Reference
For List, Dictionary, and Structure types, Get Custom Variable returns a reference, not a copy.
Modifications via reference do not trigger When Custom Variable Changes. Use Set to trigger events.
